topology_summary: 6-node mixed (5 Debian linux + 1 macOS aux) on 192.168.0.0/24; admin exit-1 + 5 clients; legacy bash orchestrator path
notes: Profile schema fix landed in a2c0b14: setup_live_lab now passes. discover_local_utm / preflight / prepare_source_archive / verify_ssh_reachability / prime_remote_access / cleanup_hosts all pass. bootstrap_hosts: 5/6 nodes (all Debian) PASS, aux (macOS client-3) FAIL. macOS aux completed install (rustynetd binary placed, launchd jobs loaded, helper socket present) but rn_bootstrap_macos.sh observed that /private/var/run/rustynet/rustynetd.sock did not appear within 40s of bootstrap completion. New failure mode (different from retry35 EPERM at enforce_baseline_runtime); EPERM fix from 66184f4 is still NOT exercised because bootstrap_hosts failed before enforce_baseline_runtime ran. Run used legacy bash orchestrator (scripts/e2e/live_linux_lab_orchestrator.sh) with --source-mode origin-main, so the Rust auto-write code committed in 7d56062 did not run on this attempt.
regression_notes: Different failure mode from retry35 (EPERM at enforce_baseline_runtime, 6d3fd1f) and retry36 (setup schema, 66184f4 against pre-a2c0b14). This is a macOS-aux-only bootstrap timeout: 40s socket-wait insufficient OR daemon failed to start. Investigate launchctl print system/com.rustynet.daemon on aux to confirm whether the daemon is loaded but not running, or running but the socket path differs from /private/var/run/rustynet/rustynetd.sock.

notes: retry41: bootstrap_hosts PASS (route-add fix 27b0e39 confirmed working; macOS aux got default route). validate_baseline_runtime PASS (Phase 24 MET). live_anchor FAIL rc=78 "invalid token material shape: empty token". topology_summary: 6-node mixed (5 Debian linux + 1 macOS aux) on 192.168.0.0/24; admin exit-1 + 5 clients; legacy bash orchestrator path
notes: retry44: FAIL bootstrap_hosts. exit debian@192.168.0.200 and aux mac@192.168.0.210 both failed DNS resolution (Could not resolve host: index.crates.io). Root cause on Linux: repair_bootstrap_dns_state wrote 1.1.1.1 through /etc/resolv.conf symlink into stub-resolv.conf; systemd-resolved regenerated it mid-build reverting to broken 127.0.0.53. Root cause on macOS: wait_for_cargo_registry_endpoint returned 0 immediately when curl succeeded without first setting 1.1.1.1 via repair. Fixed in 7316853: rm -f symlink before writing resolv.conf; always call repair_bootstrap_dns_state_macos before first curl check. Note: env_logger fix (1c254ff) not exercised; bootstrap failed before live_anchor ran.
